examples.title: Linear Search in Array
examples.description: Program to search an element in an array using linear search algorithm
examples.explanation: Linear search traverses each element sequentially until the key is found or array ends. Time complexity O(n) in worst case, O(1) in best case (element at first position).

examples.title: Binary Search in Array
examples.description: Program to search an element in a sorted array using binary search algorithm
examples.explanation: Binary search works on sorted arrays by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half. Requires sorted array. Time complexity O(log n), much faster than linear search for large arrays.

examples.title: Matrix Addition
examples.description: Program to add two 3×3 matrices using 2D arrays
examples.explanation: Uses nested loops to access each element of 2D arrays. Row-major storage allows accessing elements using arr[i][j] where i is row index and j is column index.

examples.title: Bubble Sort
examples.description: Program to sort array elements in ascending order using bubble sort algorithm
examples.explanation: Bubble sort repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if in wrong order. After each pass, largest unsorted element bubbles up. Optimized with swapped flag.
